nodes.label: IMPORTANT: This route must NOT use UserAuthenticate middleware.
nodes.norm_label: important: this route must not use userauthenticate middleware.

nodes.label: NOTE: value must be the raw text to display — MSG91 does NOT want
nodes.norm_label: note: value must be the raw text to display — msg91 does not want

nodes.label: Deploy Backend GitHub Actions Workflow
nodes.rationale: SSH deploy to a GCP VM on push to main: git pull, docker compose up --build, poll /status for health, then confirm migration status via scripts/migrate.mjs (migrations apply on app startup, deploy only verifies none pending).

nodes.label: GloUp Dev Stack
nodes.rationale: Declared stack: JWT security, Bcrypt/Crypto encryption, Sequelize ORM, MySQL, AJV validation, Multer uploads, SendMail, Express view engine, err-code, Stripe payment gateway. Note: README claims Stripe but all payment docs describe Razorpay — likely stale.

nodes.label: Partner OTP Authentication Flow
nodes.rationale: POST /partner/v2/sendotp then /partner/v2/verifyotp. Mock numbers 9876543210/9876543211 accept static OTP 123456 for testing without SMS delivery.

nodes.label: Admin Partner Approval Flow
nodes.rationale: New stores start with completion_status 'pending'; admin approves via POST /admin/app/getpartnerverification/:id setting completion_status 'completed'.

nodes.label: Store Visibility Gate
nodes.rationale: New stores must NOT appear in user-app search or nearby results until admin approval — gating prevents unvetted salons from being bookable.

nodes.label: Partner Token (AES-encrypted JWT)
nodes.rationale: Session token returned by /partner/v2/verifyotp, sent as 'partnertoken' header; invalidated by /partner/auth/logout.

nodes.label: MySQL db Service (docker-compose)
nodes.rationale: mysql:8.0 with healthcheck; app waits for service_healthy. Root password committed in the compose file — flagged as a secrets-management risk by the scale readiness review.

nodes.label: Gloup API app Service (docker-compose)
nodes.rationale: Node API on port 5678, healthchecked via GET /status, wired to MySQL, Redis (REDIS_URL redis://gloup-redis:6379), Firebase credentials, Apple auth key, and GCS bucket for images.

nodes.label: Admin Send Targeted Notification API
nodes.rationale: POST /admin/app/send-targeted-notification sends FCM push to ONE user or partner. In-app notification log is always written; push is best-effort — missing FCM token returns HTTP 201, not 400, so the recipient still sees the message in-app.

nodes.label: Admin Broadcast Notification API
nodes.rationale: POST /admin/app/addnotification targets many users/partners (sent_to: all/user/store), in contrast to the single-recipient targeted API.

nodes.label: FCM Token Pruning
nodes.rationale: Invalid/stale FCM tokens are removed from the database after failed sends (e.g. messaging/registration-token-not-registered). Only the latest stored token per recipient is used to avoid duplicate notifications on the same phone.

nodes.label: Device ID (FCM Token) Registration
nodes.rationale: POST /user/auth/deviceId and /partner/auth/deviceId register FCM tokens; each call replaces the previous token so one active token is kept per user/store.

nodes.label: API Scale Readiness Review (100k Users)
nodes.rationale: Verdict: 100k registered users is plausible after index/query cleanup; 100k concurrent users is not, given a single Node process, 20-connection DB pool, and SQL-heavy live endpoints. All fixes deliberately preserve the frontend API contract.

nodes.label: Unbounded Admin Queries
nodes.rationale: Admin endpoints (getallusers, getpayoutlogs, getallpartner, etc. in AdminDbController.js) return full tables with no pagination, causing memory blowup and table scans at scale. Fix: default server-side pagination with capped limits while keeping response shape.

nodes.label: Runtime DB Sync In Production
nodes.rationale: Production boot runs connection.sync({force:false}) in src/core/database/initialize.js — schema changes at runtime, startup races between instances, unversioned schema. Recommended fix: versioned migrations run as a deploy step.

nodes.label: Database Index Strategy
nodes.rationale: Recommends composite indexes on User, UserSession, Store, StoreSession, appointments, appointment_items, Reviews, Favourites, PartnerAddress, StoreServices, WalletLogs, NotificationLogs — the highest-leverage scale fix requiring no API change.

nodes.label: Notification Queueing (Background Workers)
nodes.rationale: FirebaseService.notifyOrderStatus sends FCM multicast directly in request paths and admin flows load all device tokens into memory. Fix: queue jobs (BullMQ+Redis or similar) so the API returns fast and workers batch sends.

nodes.label: Idempotent Payment Finalization
nodes.rationale: Duplicate paymentsucssess calls can double-apply wallet/notification side effects; payment update must check existing status/payment id and run in a transaction. Critical-priority fix.

nodes.label: Redis-Backed Rate Limiting
nodes.rationale: Current rate limits on OTP routes are in-memory, so they break across multiple app instances. Redis-backed limits needed on OTP, login, payment, search, report, and upload endpoints before horizontal scaling.

nodes.label: Observability (Metrics, Logs, APM)
nodes.rationale: Recommends request ids, structured JSON logs, latency/error/pool metrics, and APM; notes the /status endpoint only returns a static response and pool stats are noisily console-logged every 5 seconds.

nodes.label: Booking and Payment Flow
nodes.rationale: End-to-end explanation of customer booking: amount calculated server-side from DB prices, Razorpay order created, appointment + items rows written in a transaction, app-driven payment success plus Razorpay webhook as server-to-server backup.

nodes.label: V2 Create Order Flow (createOrderV2)
nodes.rationale: POST /user/app/v2/createorder: prices fetched from StoreServices/Combo by ID so the client cannot spoof amounts; gst is added as a flat amount (not a percentage); appointment saved with payment_status 'pending' but status 'booked' before payment — a critical flaw since unpaid orders occupy slots.

nodes.label: V2 Payment Success Flow (paymentsuccessV2)
nodes.rationale: POST /user/app/v2/paymentsuccess finds appointment by razorpay_id + user_id, sets payment_status 'success' / status 'booked', stores razorpay_signature WITHOUT verifying it, does not recheck paid amount, and does not credit store wallet (unlike webhook and legacy v1 — inconsistent settlement).

nodes.label: Razorpay Webhook Handler
nodes.rationale: POST /user/webhook/razorpay exists as server-to-server backup when the app never calls payment success. Verifies HMAC signature against RZ_WEBHOOK_SECRET using raw body (mounted before express.json). payment.captured credits store wallet; payment.failed writes status 'failed' which is not in the model enum and can fail; missing secret still returns 200 so Razorpay stops retrying.

nodes.label: appointments Table
nodes.rationale: Central booking table: user_id, store_id, booking_date, slot_id, amount, razorpay_id, payment_status, payment_id, razorpay_signature, status (booked/cancelled/completed/confirmed/refunded/pending), wallet/discount fields, booking_for, guest_id. Status spellings are inconsistent (success vs legacy 'sucssess').

nodes.label: appointment_items Table
nodes.rationale: Line items per booking: appointment_id plus service_id or combo_id with service_amount.

nodes.label: Slot Availability Logic
nodes.rationale: GET /user/app/v2/getslotstatus counts booked/confirmed/completed and recent (<5 min) pending appointments plus SlotBlockedDates. Because v2 creates unpaid orders as 'booked', abandoned checkouts block slots indefinitely; no row locking or unique constraint prevents two users booking the same slot concurrently.

nodes.label: Refund Flow
nodes.rationale: User initiaterefund cancels and conditionally creates refund_requests (time condition looks inverted vs normal policy). Admin updaterefundrequest triggers real Razorpay refund; admin refundbookings route is broken by a middleware function-name mismatch and a Razorpay-refund TODO. Partner cancellation never triggers refund logic.

nodes.label: Missing Razorpay Signature Verification
nodes.rationale: Critical loophole: v2 payment success stores but never verifies razorpay_signature (HMAC_SHA256 of order_id|payment_id with key secret), and never rechecks amount/currency with Razorpay — anyone can mark their own pending appointment paid. Legacy verification code exists but is commented out.

nodes.label: Partner V2 Services API
nodes.rationale: POST /v2/addservices, PATCH /v2/updateservices, GET /v2/services under /partner/app. Ownership enforced via store_id from the partnertoken session; duration strings like '1h'/'30m' normalized to HH:MM:SS.

nodes.label: Swagger / OpenAPI Documentation
nodes.rationale: OpenAPI spec is generated in-process at startup by src/core/swagger/parseRoutes.js reading the six Express route files; auth requirements inferred from middleware names; no extra npm packages — Swagger UI loaded from CDN. Named request schemas live in schemas.js/resolveRequestBody.js.

nodes.label: Legacy /createstore (v1)
nodes.rationale: Critically broken: partnerDbController.app.addpartner uses an undefined variable 'data' in its where clause and ignores the passed store id, so the update targets wrong rows or crashes. Report recommends deprecating v1 entirely.

nodes.label: Modernized /v2/createstore
nodes.rationale: Recommended path: strict field validation, atomic Sequelize transaction (upsertStoreProfile) covering address sync, category refresh via bulkCreate, and profile update — rollback prevents orphaned data. Correctly uses store ID for updates.

nodes.label: completion_status Transition Discrepancy
nodes.rationale: v2 sets completion_status 'completed' immediately on creation (partnerDbController.js:2941), but the business requirement is 'pending' until administrative approval; v1 relies on the model default 'pending'. This conflicts with the admin approval / visibility-gate workflow.

nodes.label: Umzug Database Migrations
nodes.rationale: Versioned, idempotent migrations (Umzug + SequelizeMeta, same approach as Sequelize CLI) applied automatically on app startup and via scripts/migrate.mjs. connection.sync only creates brand-new tables; ANY change to an existing table must be a migration. Helpers in migrationHelpers.js (e.g. addColumnIfMissing) keep re-runs safe when SQL was already applied manually.

nodes.label: Prometheus gloup-node-api Scrape Job
nodes.rationale: Scrapes app:5678 every 5 seconds for API metrics.

nodes.label: StoreLanguages Table (SQL)
nodes.rationale: Join table mapping stores to languages with FK cascade to Store and Languages plus a unique (store_id, language_id) constraint.

nodes.label: GuestDetails Table (SQL)
nodes.rationale: Stores guest person details (name, gender, age, phone) for bookings made on someone else's behalf; the same file ALTERs appointments to add booking_for and guest_id columns linking to it.

nodes.label: Subscription Module Tables (SQL)
nodes.rationale: SubscriptionPlans (Starter/Growth/Premium at 299/499/699 for 30 days) and StoreSubscription with FK fk_store_subscription_plan; includes a paymet_status→payment_status typo fix.

nodes.label: Legacy SQL Migration Notes
nodes.rationale: Warns not to run legacy SQL by hand: the customer-contact and pending-status appointment schema changes were converted to idempotent Umzug JS migrations that detect manual application and only record themselves in SequelizeMeta.

nodes.label: Appointment Confirmation Email Template
nodes.rationale: EJS/HTML email rendering booking confirmation: orders.order_id, orders.Finalcart service rows (service_name, amount, discounted_amount, final_price), subtotal/discount/total, and customer name. Filename 'delivered' suggests it was repurposed from an e-commerce shipping template (shipping-truck illustration remains).

nodes.label: Team Task Assignments
nodes.rationale: Informal task list: saloon web login/timeslot fix (Nanda), saloon onboarding in admin panel (DV), user app bug fixes (Alex/Hamja), hiring module (Nikhil & Jega), partner saloon services API fixes (Surya, Kuralarasan, Kavya).
